steps.prompt: Which word is a verbal form?

hints.perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle would be 'laborati' — '-antes' marks present active
hints.future participle: Future participle would be 'laboraturi' — '-antes' marks present active
hints.gerundive: Gerundive would be 'laborandi' — '-antes' marks present active participle

hints.labor, labi, lapsus sum, —: These are principal parts of 'labor' (to slip/slide) — a deponent verb
hints.libero, liberare, liberavi, liberatum: These are principal parts of 'libero' (to free)
hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are principal parts of 'laudo' (to praise)

hints.nominative: 'Praeceptor' is nominative in form, but here it is used as a direct address (vocative)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'praeceptoris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'praeceptoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'praeceptoris' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'nox' — 'noctem' with the -em ending is accusative singular
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'nocte' — 'noctem' ending in -em is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'noctis' — 'noctem' ending in -em is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'noctis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'noctis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'noctis' ends in -is

translation: Master, toiling through the whole night
